title: The One Where Nana Dies Twice

jokes.text: Dehydrated Japanese noodles under fluorescent lights... does it get better than this?
jokes.setup: Character appears to be asking genuinely about something good
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's sarcastically describing something terrible
jokes.explanation: Strong sarcastic opening with good specificity and economy - paints a vivid picture of mundane misery

jokes.text: Ah, you see, perfect might be a problem. Had you said 'co-dependent', or 'self-destructive'...
jokes.setup: Friend offers to set him up with someone 'perfect'
jokes.punchline: He admits he's attracted to dysfunction instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-driven humor with strong self-awareness and perfect comedic timing

jokes.text: He's a he?
jokes.setup: Being told about someone who would be perfect for him
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's a man, revealing the misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection with perfect economy - two words that completely reframe the situation

jokes.text: Oh God. I- just- I thought- Good, Shelley. I'm just gonna go flush myself down the toilet now...
jokes.setup: Realizing she made an embarrassing assumption
jokes.punchline: Threatens comically dramatic self-punishment
jokes.explanation: Standard embarrassment humor with predictable hyperbolic reaction

jokes.text: Um... yeah.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking if it's ridiculous someone thought he was gay
jokes.punchline: Monica's hesitant agreement reveals she thought so too
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - the pause and hesitation conveys more than the words

jokes.text: Yeah, but then you spent Phoebe's entire birthday party talking to my breasts, so then I figured maybe not.
jokes.setup: Admitting she initially thought Chandler might be gay
jokes.punchline: His obvious heterosexual behavior changed her mind
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion with specific, character-driven evidence. Great comedic logic.

jokes.text: No, it's just because, uh, I kinda wanted to go out with her too, so I told her, actually, you were seeing Bernie Spellman...
jokes.setup: Asked if he told Susan that Chandler wasn't gay
jokes.punchline: Reveals he lied to eliminate competition for the girl
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Ross's competitive side and willingness to deceive

jokes.text: WHAT IS IT?!
jokes.setup: Everyone being vague about his mysterious 'quality'
jokes.punchline: His frustrated outburst at the non-answers
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of frustration, though the yelling is somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Oh, oh, a quality, good, because I was worried you guys were gonna be vague about this.
jokes.setup: Friends continue to give vague non-answers about his 'quality'
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically thanks them for being specific when they're being anything but
jokes.explanation: Strong sarcastic delivery with perfect ironic timing - very quotable

jokes.text: I'm talking to Rome. I'm talking to Rome.
jokes.setup: Taking a call from Rachel's Italian boyfriend Paolo
jokes.punchline: Her excited repetition shows she's starstruck by the international call
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor showing Monica's tendency to get excited about status

jokes.text: So he's calling from Rome. I could do that. Just gotta go to Rome.
jokes.setup: Hearing about Paolo calling from Rome
jokes.punchline: Deadpan observation that calling from Rome just requires going to Rome
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan humor with logical but absurd solution

jokes.text: What? Nothing. Oh, maybe that's it.
jokes.setup: Asking what's different about Monica's hair
jokes.punchline: Realizes 'nothing' different might be the problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant passive-aggressive mom humor - the insult disguised as realization

jokes.text: Not just restaurants, from our house.
jokes.setup: Ross mentioning Nana stole Sweet 'n' Low from restaurants
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals she even stole them from her own family
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the grandmother's compulsive behavior

jokes.text: She's not- past, she's present, she's back.
jokes.setup: Explaining that Nana didn't actually die
jokes.punchline: Using verb tenses to explain death status
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay using grammatical tenses to discuss life and death

jokes.text: We're looking into it.
jokes.setup: Family asking if Nana has died
jokes.punchline: Doctor treats death like a bureaucratic investigation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan bureaucratic language applied to death - absurdly understated

jokes.text: This almost never happens!
jokes.setup: Patient dying twice
jokes.punchline: Nurse casually mentions this rare occurrence
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor - 'almost never' for something that should be impossible

jokes.text: Yeah, you have homosexual hair.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking if it's his hair that makes people think he's gay
jokes.punchline: Ross confirms with an absurd but matter-of-fact diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific and quotable - treating sexual orientation as a hair condition

jokes.text: Twice? Oh, that sucks!
jokes.setup: Learning that Nana died twice
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual understatement about this traumatic situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey response - treating extraordinary events with casual simplicity

jokes.text: We checked. A lot.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggesting maybe Nana isn't really gone
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact confirmation of death verification
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good understatement - 'a lot' suggests obsessive death-checking

jokes.text: I always get this really strong Debbie vibe whenever I use one of those little yellow pencils, you know?
jokes.setup: Talking about feeling dead people's presence
jokes.punchline: Associates her dead friend with golf pencils
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - connecting death by lightning on golf course to pencils

jokes.text: Sure. I just sharpened her this morning.
jokes.setup: Being offered a pencil after talking about dead friend Debbie
jokes.punchline: Refers to the pencil as 'her' like it's Debbie
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with perfect character logic - treating pencil as reincarnated friend

jokes.text: Now, see, I don't believe any of that. I think once you're dead, you're dead! You're gone! You're worm food!
jokes.setup: Phoebe's spiritual talk about feeling dead people's presence
jokes.punchline: Ross's blunt materialist counter-argument
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing Ross's scientific materialism vs Phoebe's spirituality

jokes.text: So Chandler looks gay, huh?
jokes.setup: Heavy conversation about death and afterlife
jokes.punchline: Ross abruptly changes to trivial topic about Chandler's appearance
jokes.explanation: Great comedic timing - deflating profound moment with petty observation

jokes.text: You know, I don't know who this is, but it's not Debbie.
jokes.setup: Using a pencil she claimed was connected to dead friend Debbie
jokes.punchline: Casually admits this particular pencil isn't giving her Debbie vibes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe logic - matter-of-factly distinguishing between pencil spirits

jokes.text: Here's my retainer!
jokes.setup: Monica is doing something with Nana's appearance in the casket
jokes.punchline: She accidentally drops her retainer into the casket
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy at inappropriate moment - funeral setting makes it more awkward

jokes.text: You what? I wanna be buried at sea, it looks like fun.
jokes.setup: Father announcing his burial wishes
jokes.punchline: Describes burial at sea as 'fun' like it's a recreational activity
jokes.explanation: Absurd perspective on death - treating burial method as entertainment choice

jokes.text: And then we throw your body in the water... Gee, that does sound fun.
jokes.setup: Father describing burial at sea as a fun day out
jokes.punchline: Monica sarcastically agrees while describing the morbid reality
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response highlighting the absurdity of treating body disposal as fun

jokes.text: Everyone thinks they know me. Everyone says 'Jack Geller, so predictable'. Maybe after I'm gone, they'll say 'Buried at sea! Huh!'.
jokes.setup: Wanting to surprise people with his burial choice
jokes.punchline: Plans his posthumous reputation around burial method
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor about legacy and how we want to be remembered

jokes.text: Well, I think Brian's a little out of your league.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying he'd prefer to be set up with Brian
jokes.punchline: Shelley bluntly tells him Brian is too good for him
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - brutal honesty about attractiveness hierarchy

jokes.text: Because I could get a Brian. Believe you me. ...I'm really not.
jokes.setup: Defending his ability to attract someone like Brian
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with honest admission
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor - bravado immediately deflated by honesty

jokes.text: You know, whatever we pick, she would've told us it's the wrong one.
jokes.setup: Struggling to pick the right outfit for dead grandmother
jokes.punchline: Realizes she would have criticized any choice while alive
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about family dynamics and impossible-to-please relatives

jokes.text: And where she's going everyone else will be dressier?
jokes.setup: Funeral director saying certain shoes are too casual
jokes.punchline: Monica questions the dress code for the afterlife
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - applying fashion concerns to death and afterlife

jokes.text: Some days it's all I can think about.
jokes.setup: Mother reminding her not to wear hair up because ears aren't her best feature
jokes.punchline: Monica admits to obsessing over this criticism
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor about lasting impact of parental criticism

jokes.text: Hi, sorry I'm late, I couldn't find my bearings. Oh, you-you mean your earrings? What'd I say?
jokes.setup: Apologizing for being late
jokes.punchline: Accidentally says 'bearings' instead of 'earrings'
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe malapropism - endearing but predictable character quirk

jokes.text: It's stuff like that, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Jack Geller calling them 'dressed up' in feminine way
jokes.punchline: Chandler realizes this contributes to people thinking he's gay
jokes.explanation: Good callback with self-awareness - connecting specific moments to broader pattern

jokes.text: You know, I think it might be time for you to start using night cream.
jokes.setup: Tender moment of sympathy at the funeral
jokes.punchline: Immediately shifts to criticism about Monica's appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - mother's inability to be supportive without criticism

jokes.text: Check it out, Giants-Cowboys. You're watching a football game at a funeral? No, it's the pre-game. I'm gonna watch it at the reception.
jokes.setup: Joey apparently watching something during funeral
jokes.punchline: He's watching football pre-game and plans to continue at reception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - completely inappropriate priorities with innocent justification

jokes.text: God, what a great day. ...What? Weather-wise!
jokes.setup: At a funeral, Chandler comments on it being a great day
jokes.punchline: Quickly clarifies he means the weather, not the funeral
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment humor - inappropriate comment requiring quick clarification

jokes.text: I'm fine. Just-just... having my worst fear realised...
jokes.setup: Ross appears to have fallen into the grave
jokes.punchline: Describes falling into grave as his worst fear coming true
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with character-appropriate neurotic response

jokes.text: What, what is it? You missed a belt loop. Oh! No-n- Okay, it's in spasm.
jokes.setup: Monica checking Ross's back injury
jokes.punchline: First reveals trivial fashion mistake, then actual medical problem
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - fashion concern before medical concern shows character priorities

jokes.text: Sorry- Hi, I'm Dorothy's daughter. Hi, I'm Chandler, and I have no idea who Dorothy is.
jokes.setup: Woman introducing herself as Dorothy's daughter
jokes.punchline: Chandler's honest admission of complete ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan honesty in social situation - breaking funeral politeness norms

jokes.text: I love you guys. You guys are the greatest. I love my sister, I love Pheebs... Chandler!
jokes.setup: Ross expressing love while drugged
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic but confused declarations of affection
jokes.explanation: Standard drugged behavior comedy - predictable but character-appropriate

jokes.text: And listen, man, if you wanna be gay, be gay. Doesn't matter to me.
jokes.setup: Ross giving Chandler supportive advice while drugged
jokes.punchline: Offers acceptance for something that isn't true
jokes.explanation: Good callback with drugged Ross offering misguided support

jokes.text: Rachel. Rachel Rachel. I love you the most.
jokes.setup: Ross declaring love for everyone while drugged
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately singles out Rachel as his favorite
jokes.explanation: Predictable drugged behavior revealing hidden feelings - somewhat cliche

jokes.text: Just a, uh... hearing disability.
jokes.setup: Someone asking what Joey has (likely headphones or earbuds)
jokes.punchline: Joey euphemistically describes his football-watching equipment
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism - describing headphones as disability to avoid admitting inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: No, I'd be hearing about 'Why didn't I get the honey-glazed ham?'
jokes.setup: Saying grandmother would have hated the reception
jokes.punchline: Specific complaint the grandmother would have made
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - specific example of impossible-to-please relative

jokes.text: I didn't spend enough on flowers, and if I spent more she'd be saying 'Why are you wasting your money? I don't need flowers, I'm dead'.
jokes.setup: Continuing list of grandmother's hypothetical complaints
jokes.punchline: Shows no matter what choice was made, it would be criticized
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing impossible family dynamics - can't win either way

jokes.text: I can imagine.
jokes.setup: Mother asking if Monica knows what it's like to grow up with criticism
jokes.punchline: Monica's dry response suggests she knows exactly
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - understated response with clear subtext about inherited family pattern

jokes.text: That little naked guy would be me. Aww, look at the little thing. Yes, yes, fine, that is my penis. Can we be grown-ups now?
jokes.setup: People looking at baby photos
jokes.punchline: Ross's embarrassed admission and plea for maturity
jokes.explanation: Standard embarrassing photo humor with predictable escalation

jokes.text: Ooh, look-look-look-look-look! I got Monica naked! Nono, that would be me again. I'm, uh, just trying something.
jokes.setup: Joey excited about finding naked photo
jokes.punchline: Ross reveals it's another naked baby photo of himself
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier baby photo embarrassment - predictable but maintains running gag

jokes.text: It's like Mardi Gras without the paper mache heads.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking about working in Financial Services
jokes.punchline: Lowell's sarcastic description of the boring department
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor for boring office work - specific and highly quotable

jokes.text: Pretty much, most of the time. We have a kind of... radar.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking if Lowell can tell he's not gay
jokes.punchline: Lowell explains gaydar as a real phenomenon
jokes.explanation: Straightforward explanation of gaydar concept - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Speaking for my people, I'd have to say no.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking if he has 'a quality' that makes him seem gay
jokes.punchline: Lowell formally represents gay community in saying no
jokes.explanation: Good callback with formal diplomatic language - 'speaking for my people' is nicely absurd

jokes.text: He is? Yup, and way out of your league.
jokes.setup: Learning that Brian from Payroll is actually gay
jokes.punchline: Lowell confirms he's also too attractive for Chandler
jokes.explanation: Good callback confirming earlier brutal assessment about Chandler's attractiveness level

jokes.text: Out of my league. I could get a Brian. If I wanted to get a Brian, I could get a Brian. Hey, Brian.
jokes.setup: Being told Brian is out of his league
jokes.punchline: Chandler's repeated insistence followed by awkward attempt to prove it
jokes.explanation: Escalation of earlier insecurity with cringe ending - predictable but effective

tmdb_overview: The group mourns Monica and Ross's grandmother; Chandler faces workplace rumors about his sexual orientation.
